Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance across the application. ## Why This Matters - Legal requirement in many jurisdictions - Better UX for all users - Improves SEO and usability - From README: "We optimize for an elite UX" ## Accessibility Requirements ### 1. Keyboard Navigation - All interactive elements focusable via Tab - Focus indicators visible and high-contrast - Logical tab order following visual layout - Keyboard shortcuts documented - No keyboard traps (can always escape modals) ### 2. Screen Reader Support - Semantic HTML elements (nav, main, aside, etc.) - ARIA labels on interactive elements - ARIA live regions for dynamic content - Meaningful alt text for images - Form labels associated with inputs ### 3. Color & Contrast - 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text - 3:1 contrast ratio for large text - Color not sole indicator (use icons + text) - Focus indicators meet contrast requirements - Dark mode meets same requirements ### 4. Forms & Inputs - Labels visible and associated - Error messages clear and accessible - Required fields indicated - Validation errors announced - Focus moves to first error on submit ### 5. Responsive & Zoom - Content readable at 200% zoom - No horizontal scrolling at 320px viewport - Touch targets at least 44x44px on mobile - Text resizes without breaking layout ## Implementation ### Focus Management ```typescript // src/lib/focus.ts export function focusTrap(container: HTMLElement) { /* ... */ } export function moveFocusTo(element: HTMLElement) { /* ... */ } export function announceToScreenReader(message: string) { /* ... */ } ``` ### Skip Link ```typescript // In layout.tsx Skip to main content
``` ### ARIA Live Region ```typescript // src/components/ui/live-region.tsx export function LiveRegion({ message }: { message: string }) { return (
{message}
); } ``` ### Accessibility Audit Component ```typescript // In development only import { axe } from '@axe-core/react'; if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') { axe(React, ReactDOM, 1000); } ``` ## Testing Requirements - Automated tests with axe-core - Manual testing with VoiceOver/NVDA - Keyboard-only navigation testing - Color contrast checking - Responsive testing at various sizes ## Files to Create - `src/lib/accessibility/focus.ts` - `src/lib/accessibility/announce.ts` - `src/components/ui/skip-link.tsx` - `src/components/ui/live-region.tsx` - `e2e/accessibility/a11y.spec.ts` ## Dependencies ```json { "@axe-core/react": "^4.0.0", "@axe-core/playwright": "^4.0.0" } ``` ## Checklist by Component Type ### Buttons - [ ] Has accessible name (text or aria-label) - [ ] Focus visible - [ ] Keyboard activatable ### Modals/Dialogs - [ ] Focus trapped - [ ] Escape closes - [ ] Focus returns on close - [ ] role="dialog" + aria-labelledby ### Forms - [ ] Labels associated - [ ] Errors announced - [ ] Required indicated - [ ] Autocomplete attributes ## Success Criteria - Passes axe automated testing - Keyboard navigation works throughout - Screen reader announces all content - Color contrast meets requirements - Focus management works correctly ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:56.113962088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:56.113962088-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ear","title":"Implement hash embedder fallback (search/hash-embedder.ts) -- Details: hashEmbed(input, dims=128) creates deterministic vector via fast hash. Used when MiniLM unavailable. Provides approximate similarity without model download. No external deps.","notes":"Added deterministic hash embedder fallback and exported it","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:44.255150843-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:32:43.538655859-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:32:43.538658875-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: hashEmbedding(text, dims=128) in semantic.ts using LSH with char n-grams. Verified working: deterministic, 128 dims, normalized vectors. Exported from search/index.ts."} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ehsz","title":"Implement spam prevention for community content","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7g3z. ## Task Implement spam prevention mechanisms for user-generated content. ## Why This Matters - Protect quality of Swap Meet - Prevent abuse of platform - Maintain user trust - Legal protection ## Spam Prevention Layers ### 1. Rate Limiting - Max prompts per hour: 10 - Max prompts per day: 50 - Burst protection - Per-user limits ### 2. Content Filtering - Block known spam patterns - URL spam detection - Excessive capitalization - Repeated characters - Known bad words ### 3. New User Restrictions - First 24 hours: limited posting - First 7 days: moderation queue - Earn trust over time ### 4. Automated Detection - AI-based spam scoring (optional) - Pattern matching - Similarity to known spam - Link density analysis ### 5. User Reporting - Report spam button - Quick review queue - User reputation impact ### 6. Account Health Score - Based on: reports, quality, engagement - Low score = more scrutiny - High score = less friction ## Implementation ### Content Check Service ```typescript interface SpamCheckResult { isSpam: boolean; confidence: number; reasons: string[]; requiresReview: boolean; } async function checkContent(content: string, userId: string): Promise ``` ### Moderation Queue - Content flagged for review - Admin approve/reject - Learn from decisions ## Database ```typescript export const contentFlags = pgTable("content_flags", { id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(), contentId: text("content_id").notNull(), contentType: text("content_type").notNull(), flagType: text("flag_type").notNull(), // spam, abuse, copyright reportedBy: uuid("reported_by").references(() => userProfiles.id), status: text("status").default("pending"), reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"), createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ## Files to Create - src/lib/moderation/spam-check.ts - src/lib/moderation/content-filter.ts - src/app/api/moderation/report/route.ts - src/components/moderation/report-button.tsx ## Success Criteria - Spam blocked effectively - False positives low - Legitimate users unaffected - Easy to review/appeal ## UX & Fairness Additions - Provide transparent feedback when content is flagged (reason + appeal path). - Add soft-block state for borderline content (visible to author only). - Log moderation decisions for model tuning and dispute handling. ## Test Coverage - Unit: spam scoring thresholds + pattern filters - Integration: moderation queue creation + review actions - E2E: jeffreysprompts_premium-gx6z (spam + reporting + moderation) ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:50.374169205-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:50.374169205-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ehsz","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ro6f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.307523545-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ehsz","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.434463797-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ehsz","title":"Implement spam prevention for community content","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7g3z. ## Task Implement spam prevention mechanisms for user-generated content. ## Why This Matters - Protect quality of Swap Meet - Prevent abuse of platform - Maintain user trust - Legal protection ## Spam Prevention Layers ### 1. Extended thinking accordion. Code block syntax highlighting (react-syntax-highlighter). ToolCallDisplay with expandable input/output. Highlight annotation cards.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:27:18.08143194-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:27.828979131-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:27.828979131-05:00","close_reason":"MessageDetail component already implemented","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-evp","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:38:11.069527589-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","title":"Implement changelog and what's new feature","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xj4e. ## Task Implement a changelog to communicate new features and updates to users. ## Why This Matters - Users know about new features - Reduces "I didn't know you could do that" - Shows product is actively developed - Marketing opportunity ## Components ### 1. Changelog Page - /changelog - Public page - Chronological list of updates - Categories: New, Improved, Fixed - Date for each entry - SEO-friendly ### 2. What's New Modal - Shown to users after update - Highlights major changes - Dismissible (remember dismissal) - Link to full changelog ### 3. Update Badge - Small badge on nav item - "1 new update" indicator - Clears when viewed ### 4. Email Digest (optional) - Monthly changelog email - Major updates only - For engaged users ## Changelog Entry Format ```markdown ## March 2026 ### New - **Collections Sharing**: Share entire collections with a link - **Dark Mode**: Toggle between light and dark themes ### Improved - Search is now 2x faster - Better mobile navigation ### Fixed - Fixed issue with export failing for large collections - Corrected billing date display ``` ## Database ```typescript export const changelogEntries = pgTable("changelog_entries", { id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(), version: text("version"), // optional semver title: text("title").notNull(), content: text("content").notNull(), // markdown category: text("category").notNull(), // new, improved, fixed publishedAt: timestamp("published_at"), createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), }); // Track which users have seen which entries export const changelogViews = pgTable("changelog_views", { userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id), lastSeenEntryId: uuid("last_seen_entry_id"), seenAt: timestamp("seen_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ## Files to Create - src/app/(public)/changelog/page.tsx - src/components/changelog/whats-new-modal.tsx - src/components/changelog/update-badge.tsx - content/changelog/*.mdx - src/app/api/changelog/route.ts ## Success Criteria - Changelog updated regularly - Users notified of changes - Easy to maintain - SEO-friendly ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:14.107049999-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:59:54.703436949-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:59:54.703436949-05:00","close_reason":"Changelog page implemented at /changelog with version history, categorized changes, timeline design, and TypeScript-native data structure."} @@ -265,7 +265,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization ## Background & Problem Statement The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css: - text-xs (12px) - text-sm (14px) - text-base (16px) - text-lg (18px) - text-xl+ (headings) However, several components still use arbitrary values like \`text-[11px]\` and \`text-[10px]\`, which: 1. Break the design system's rhythm 2. Are harder to maintain 3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements ## Files Affected 1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px] 2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px] ## The Fix Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size: - \`text-[11px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference) - \`text-[10px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable) ## Implementation Note **Typography fixes are included in the color fix tasks:** - g0oe (PromptDetailModal) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section - usn5 (SpotlightSearch) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section This keeps related changes together (same lines of code) and reduces PR churn. ## Design Rationale - 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading - The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts - Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case ## Success Criteria - [ ] Zero instances of \`text-[Npx]\` in component files - [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes - [ ] No layout shifts from size changes - [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes ## Validation The color validation tests (tzmc) include a typography consistency check that will verify no arbitrary text sizes remain. ## Dependencies - Typography fixes are bundled into g0oe and usn5 tasks - tzmc validates the result ## Estimated Scope - 2 files (via g0oe and usn5) - 7 class replacements - Minimal testing required","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:27:01.282517492-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l35x","title":"E2E Tests: Admin Dashboard & Moderation","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-whb. ## Task Create comprehensive E2E tests for the admin dashboard with detailed logging. ## Test Scenarios ### Admin Access Tests 1. **Access Control** - Non-admin user cannot access /admin - Admin user can access /admin - Admin role is verified on each request - Tampering with role fails ### User Management Tests 2. **User List** - List all users with pagination - Search users by email/name - Filter by tier (free/premium) - Filter by status (active/suspended/deleted) - Sort by join date, name, etc. 3. **User Detail** - View user profile details - View subscription history - View content (prompts, packs, skills) - View activity log 4. **User Actions** - Suspend user account - Unsuspend user account - Grant premium manually - Revoke premium - Delete user (soft delete) - Impersonate user (admin debug) ### Content Moderation Tests 5. **Reported Content** - View reported content queue - See report details and reason - View the reported content - Approve content (dismiss reports) - Remove content from Swap Meet - Warn content creator - Ban repeat offenders 6. **Featured Content** - Mark prompt as featured - Set "Prompt of the Day" - Manage featured carousel - Schedule featured content ### Analytics Tests 7. **Dashboard Metrics** - Total users (by tier) - New signups (daily/weekly/monthly) - Active users - Subscription revenue (MRR) - Churn rate - Content metrics (prompts, packs, skills) 8. **Export Data** - Export user list to CSV - Export revenue reports - Export content reports ### System Management Tests 9. **Announcements** - Create system announcement - Set announcement visibility - Schedule announcement - Dismiss announcement 10. **Maintenance Mode** - Enable maintenance mode - Users see maintenance page - Admins can still access - Disable maintenance mode ## Logging Requirements - Log all admin actions with admin ID - Log before/after state for user changes - Log moderation decisions with reason - Log impersonation sessions - Screenshot admin dashboard states - Log analytics query performance ## Test Infrastructure - Create test admin user - Create test regular users to manage - Seed reported content for moderation - Clean up test data after suite ## Files to Create - `e2e/admin/access.spec.ts` - `e2e/admin/user-management.spec.ts` - `e2e/admin/content-moderation.spec.ts` - `e2e/admin/analytics.spec.ts` - `e2e/admin/system.spec.ts` - `e2e/lib/admin-helpers.ts` ## Success Criteria - Admin access properly restricted - User management flows work - Moderation actions apply correctly - Analytics display accurate data - Audit trail is complete ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:11.578420397-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:24:11.578420397-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l35x","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-gn9y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:36.326630826-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l4b","title":"Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline -- Background: Production-grade CI ensures code quality and secure binary distribution. Workflow: test/lint/build on every PR, release workflow on tags builds CLI binaries for all platforms (linux-x64/arm64, darwin-x64/arm64, windows-x64), creates GitHub Release with SHA256SUMS.txt for verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:55.789955358-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:41.880650109-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:41.880650109-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: CI workflow (lint/test/build/typecheck) and release workflow with multi-platform binaries and SHA256SUMS"} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","title":"EPIC: Content Reporting & Moderation","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5ly. ## Overview Content reporting and moderation workflow for community safety. ## Report Flow (User Side) ### 1. Report Button - Available on all public content (prompts, packs, skills) - Icon button (flag) with "Report" tooltip - Only for logged-in users - Can't report own content ### 2. Report Dialog - Reason selection: - Spam or misleading - Inappropriate content - Copyright violation - Other (with text field) - Optional additional details - Submit button ### 3. Confirmation - "Report submitted" toast - "We'll review this within 24 hours" ## Moderation Flow (Admin Side) ### 1. Moderation Queue - List of pending reports - Sort by: date, report count, severity - Filter by: status, type, reason ### 2. Review Interface - View reported content - View reporter info and reason - View report history for this content - Actions: - Dismiss report (no action) - Warn author - Remove content - Ban user ### 3. Actions - Remove: Set visibility to 'removed', notify author - Warn: Send warning email, record strike - Ban: Suspend account, notify user ## Database Schema ```typescript export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", { id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(), reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull(), contentType: text("content_type").notNull(), contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(), reason: text("reason").notNull(), details: text("details"), status: text("status").default("pending"), // pending, reviewed, actioned, dismissed reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"), reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"), action: text("action"), // none, warned, removed, banned createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:24.60921929-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:24.60921929-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","title":"EPIC: Content Reporting & Moderation","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5ly. ## Overview Content reporting and moderation workflow for community safety. ## Report Flow (User Side) ### 1. Report Button - Available on all public content (prompts, packs, skills) - Icon button (flag) with "Report" tooltip - Only for logged-in users - Can't report own content ### 2. Report Dialog - Reason selection: - Spam or misleading - Inappropriate content - Copyright violation - Other (with text field) - Optional additional details - Submit button ### 3. Confirmation - "Report submitted" toast - "We'll review this within 24 hours" ## Moderation Flow (Admin Side) ### 1. Moderation Queue - List of pending reports - Sort by: date, report count, severity - Filter by: status, type, reason ### 2. Review Interface - View reported content - View reporter info and reason - View report history for this content - Actions: - Dismiss report (no action) - Warn author - Remove content - Ban user ### 3. Actions - Remove: Set visibility to 'removed', notify author - Warn: Send warning email, record strike - Ban: Suspend account, notify user ## Database Schema ```typescript export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", { id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(), reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull(), contentType: text("content_type").notNull(), contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(), reason: text("reason").notNull(), details: text("details"), status: text("status").default("pending"), // pending, reviewed, actioned, dismissed reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"), reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"), action: text("action"), // none, warned, removed, banned createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(), }); ``` ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:18:24.60921929-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:18:39.753096893-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T12:18:39.753096893-05:00","close_reason":"Wired report submissions into shared store + admin queue, with update actions and stats"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-leo","title":"Build BundleCard component","description":"**Details:** - Displays bundle in grid with: icon, title, description, included prompt IDs - Install bundle button (copies jfp install --bundle command) - View details link to /bundles/[id] - Featured badge for featured bundles - Framer-motion animations **Props:** ```typescript interface BundleCardProps { bundle: Bundle; index: number; // for staggered animation } ``` **Component Features:** - Dynamic Lucide icon from bundle.icon field - Prompt ID badges showing included prompts - Install button with toast feedback - Hover effects matching PromptCard style **Location:** apps/web/src/components/bundle-card.tsx **Reasoning:** Bundles need visual representation on /bundles page and anywhere bundles are displayed. Consistent with PromptCard design language.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:33:24.274352693-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:55:27.827886317-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:55:27.827886317-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented BundleCard component: dynamic Lucide icons via iconMap, install command copy, featured badge, prompt ID badges, workflow preview, framer-motion-ready with staggered animation delay","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-leo","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:56.265126492-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-lf6","title":"Build InsightCard component for transcript highlights","description":"Part 10.5 of PLAN.
